behaviors.description: Renamed files appear only in the rename category, not as delete+add.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: This failure is at the CLI↔git boundary (rename detection and status encoding), not a single local function. REAL-INFRA: Correctness depends on real git subprocess behavior for rename detection, which varies by git heuristics and flags. MOCK-HIDE: A mocked diff/status payload can trivially satisfy this and miss real rename-classification failures. LLM-DEP: No AI/ML output involved. MULTI-STEP: No multi-agent pipeline. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic/Workflow rejected because behavior text is purely git/change-classification logic with no model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temporary git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI via subprocess, and assert the report increments only `renamed` while `added` and `deleted` stay unchanged for that file.

behaviors.description: Binary-file diffs do not crash the tool; binaries are counted.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure commonly occurs at subprocess output/encoding boundaries when git emits binary diff metadata. REAL-INFRA: Requires real filesystem binary content plus real git diff output format/bytes. MOCK-HIDE: Stubbed text diff output can hide decode/crash paths that appear only with real binary artifacts. LLM-DEP: No AI/ML dependency. MULTI-STEP: No multi-agent sequence. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic/Workflow rejected because behavior text concerns crash resilience/counting for git binary diffs only.
behaviors.test_description: In a real temp repo, add/modify a binary file (e.g., random bytes), run the CLI, assert process exits successfully and binary file is included in counts.

behaviors.description: Repo with no commits yields an empty report, not an error.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The bug appears when the CLI handles git's unborn-branch/no-HEAD states across module boundaries. REAL-INFRA: Needs real git repository state with no commits; git commands often return specific non-zero exits/messages. MOCK-HIDE: Mocking git responses can miss actual exit-code/error-text handling behavior. LLM-DEP: None. MULTI-STEP: None. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic/Workflow rejected because no model output or agent chain is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a real git repo with zero commits, run the CLI, assert zero exit error handling and an empty structured report on stdout.

behaviors.description: Report counts match actual repository change history.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: This validates end-to-end correctness of history extraction and categorization, spanning git query semantics and local aggregation modules. REAL-INFRA: Requires real commit history and real git command behavior over that history. MOCK-HIDE: Fixtures/stubs may match expected shape but miss edge cases in real history traversal and status interpretation. LLM-DEP: None. MULTI-STEP: None. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic/Workflow rejected because behavior is deterministic SCM accounting, not model judgment.
behaviors.test_description: Build a real repo history (adds/deletes/renames/modifies across commits), run the CLI against that repo, and compare emitted counts to independently computed ground truth from git history.

behaviors.description: Output is identical for equivalent staged vs unstaged changes.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: The failure is about git index vs working-tree semantics crossing CLI command-selection/state-handoff boundaries. REAL-INFRA: Requires real git index/worktree behavior and subprocess calls; in-process fakes do not reproduce index nuances. MOCK-HIDE: Mocked responses can force equality and hide real divergence between staged/unstaged paths. LLM-DEP: None. MULTI-STEP: None. REJECTION-GATE: Agentic/Workflow rejected because behavior text specifies SCM state equivalence, not AI-mediated decisions.
behaviors.test_description: In a real repo, create identical logical changes once as staged and once as unstaged (in controlled runs), execute CLI for each state, and assert identical structured output.
